description: A tree is 'raelu' because it rises far from base to crown. A window can also be 'raelu' when it sits high above the floor, so the word covers tall extent and high position. 'raeli' (height) is the measurable dimension; 'mulu' (low) gives the other end of the vertical scale.
articulatory_notes: Begin with a tap or short trill for 'r' and open into central 'a'. The vowel break leaves front 'e' on its own, where it takes the stress. The tongue tip then contacts the alveolar ridge for 'l' before rounded high-back 'u'.
usage_notes: 'raelu shiro' is a tall tree, while 'raelu noa' is a high position. 'teku' (short) concerns limited end-to-end length or duration.

semantic_domains.physical: height from base to top in a person or upright structure
semantic_domains.spatial: a high vertical position or extent relative to a reference level
